source: Open Trivia Database (CC BY-SA 4.0) + hand-drafted facts
note: Bootstrapped with hand-drafted exemplars across categories. Run scripts/audit-questions.py to grow toward the 200 target.

questions.prompt: The Flag of the European Union has how many stars on it?
questions.fact: The 12 stars on the EU flag don't represent member countries — 12 was chosen as a symbol of completeness, and the count never changes when countries join or leave.

questions.prompt: Antibiotics are generally taken to combat what?
questions.fact: Antibiotics only work against bacteria, not viruses, which is why doctors don't prescribe them for colds or flu.

questions.prompt: In which country is the city of Rio de Janeiro?
questions.fact: Rio de Janeiro means 'River of January' in Portuguese — explorers named the bay in January 1502, mistaking it for a river mouth.

questions.prompt: The moons, Miranda, Ariel, Umbriel, Titania and Oberon orbit which planet?
questions.fact: Uranus's moons are mostly named after characters from Shakespeare and Alexander Pope — a tradition started by astronomer John Herschel in the 1850s.

questions.prompt: Which continent do sweet potatoes originally come from?
questions.fact: Sweet potatoes were domesticated in South America at least 8,000 years ago — archaeologists have found their remains in Peruvian caves.

questions.prompt: What country hosted the 2014 Winter Olympics?
questions.fact: Sochi 2014 was the most expensive Olympics in history at the time — about $51 billion, more than every previous Winter Games combined.

questions.prompt: How many legs do butterflies have?
questions.fact: All insects have six legs, and butterflies are insects — though their long, thin front legs are easy to miss tucked against the body.
